data.title: Why Is Vanguard Total Stock Market (VTI) ETF Up Today, 4/22/26?
data.description:
The Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI), which tracks the broader U.S. stock market, rose about 0.75% at Wednesday's open. The move followed news that President Donald Trump extended a U.S. ceasefire with Iran. After Tuesday's close, he announced a two-week extension, citing Tehran's "seriously fractured" government.

Market sentiment was also supported by a strong start to earnings season. Boeing (BA) shares climbed more than 4% after the company reported a smaller-than-expected quarterly loss. Meanwhile, GE Vernova (GEV) shares jumped 10% after its first-quarter revenue beat expectations.

Overall, VTI ETF is up 5% over the past five days. Meanwhile, its three-month average trading volume stands at around 6.14 million shares.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Anthropic Opens Full Investigation Into Unconfirmed Mythos Security Breach
data.description:
Anthropic, the artificial intelligence (AI) firm behind Claude, said it is investigating unauthorized access to Mythos, its powerful new model, which the company worries could be a boon for bad actors. The incident has raised fresh concerns about the security of advanced AI tools, prompting Anthropic to sound the alarm about potential misuse by hackers in large-scale cyberattacks.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Mythos Security Breach Came Through a Vendor System

Bloomberg, which first reported the probe on April 21, said a small group of users gained access to Anthropic's unreleased Mythos AI model through a computer system normally reserved for its external vendors. According to the report, the individuals involved are members of a private Discord channel dedicated to tracking unreleased AI models. 

They exploited multiple access points to gain entry to the restricted system. One method involved credentials belonging to an employee at a contractor supporting Anthropic's operations. Another involved guessing the model's URL based on familiarity with Anthropic's naming conventions for its existing models. 

Since gaining access, members have reportedly used Mythos regularly. They later provided Bloomberg with proof in the form of screenshots and a live demonstration. Anthropic confirmed it is investigating the claims and said the access may have come "through one of our third-party vendor environments."

The company added that it found no evidence that the activity extended beyond the vendor environment used to access systems during model development. AI labs commonly rely on third-party contractors for tasks such as testing, although it remains unclear which vendor was involved in this case.

Mythos Breach Raises Concerns Over Security 

The reported unauthorized access has added to concerns surrounding Mythos. It raised questions about whether Anthropic can keep sensitive technology out of the hands of exploiters. After all, the company recently experienced two major leaks in just one week, exposing Claude Code's sensitive data and information about Mythos before its preview launch.

Anthropic has stated that Mythos significantly outperforms other AI systems in terms of vulnerability detection. Because of its capabilities, the private company withheld the model from general release, granting access to only a closed but growing group of companies and financial institutions. This includes Apple (AAPL), Google (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Nvidia (NVDA), Goldman Sachs (GS), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), and others, to test the model and strengthen their cybersecurity defenses.

Anthropic has also faced criticism from some observers who argue it may be overstating the model's capabilities as competition intensifies with rival OpenAI. Still, financial institutions in the U.K. are expected to begin using Mythos soon, while banks in Japan and Canada are in discussions about adoption. 

Which AI Stocks Are a Strong Buy?

While Anthropic remains a private company with no publicly traded stock yet, investors interested in AI stocks can explore well-known players such as Nvidia (NVDA), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL), Micron (MU), Microsoft (MSFT), and Meta Platforms (META). All of these stocks have been rated Strong Buys by various Wall Street analysts. To get more information on their performance ratings and price targets, visit TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: S&P 500 Bounces Back as Trump Says U.S.-Iran Talks 'Possible' by Friday
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) opened Wednesday higher after President Trump said the next round of U.S.-Iran peace talks could occur in the coming days.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Trump told the New York Post that it's "possible" that talks will begin by Friday, while sources in Islamabad echoed his claim, saying negotiations could begin within 36 to 72 hours. Iran hasn't responded positively to the ceasefire, at least publicly, with Iranian official Mahdi Mohammadi saying the move is "certainly a ploy to buy time for a surprise strike."

Iran Divisions Cloud Ceasefire Outlook

Trump didn't specify the duration of the ceasefire, although Axios reports that the truce could last between three to five days, citing a source familiar with the matter. Sentiment among Iranian officials is sharply divided, with the IRGC advocating for a hardline approach and negotiators pushing for renewed talks and a potential diplomatic resolution.

The IRGC previously stated that it would not commit to negotiating until the U.S. lifts its naval blockade on vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports. Trump has shown no indication of clearing the blockade.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Stock Futures Rise as Trump Extends U.S.-Iran Ceasefire; Tesla Earnings in Focus
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended higher on Wednesday morning as President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran, easing fears of an immediate escalation in the Middle East. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.93%, 0.74%, and 0.75%, respectively, at 8:30 a.m. EDT on April 22.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

Trump extended the ceasefire, saying it is needed as the Iranian government is "seriously fractured" and the country's leadership and representatives need time to present a "unified proposal" to resolve the conflict. Oil prices rose in reaction to the extension of the ceasefire. At the time of writing, the international benchmark Brent crude (CM:BZ) rose 0.60% at $99.07 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) gained 0.36% to $89.99.

Meanwhile, all eyes are on electric vehicle (EV) giant Tesla's (TSLA) first-quarter results after the market close today. Investors will mainly focus on management's commentary on the EV demand backdrop and robotaxi expansion timelines.

Major indices closed lower on Tuesday amid persistent tensions in the Middle East. The S&P 500 fell 0.63%, while both the Dow Jones and the Nasdaq Composite declined by 0.59% each.

Turning to trending stocks, GE Vernova (GEV) shares jumped about 8% in Wednesday's pre-market trading after the energy tech company announced strong Q1 results. Also, Boeing (BA) stock was up more than 4% after the aircraft maker announced a narrower-than-expected loss for the first quarter of 2026.

In contrast, Vertiv Holdings (VRT) stock fell about 3% despite the AI infrastructure company reporting market-beating Q1 results. The company's business in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA) continued to be under pressure.

Meanwhile, Adobe (ADBE) stock rose 3% in pre-market trading after the software company announced a $25 billion stock buyback program through April 2030.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Where Do Analysts Stand on GE Aerospace Stock (GE) Post Q1 Earnings?
data.description:
GE Aerospace (GE) stock saw a slight drop on Wednesday after analysts weighed in on the American aircraft company following its latest earnings report. While the latest analyst updates are still bullish, there were some price target cuts.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Let's dig into the most recent updates from Wall Street's top analysts below.

  • Five-star TD Cowen analyst Gautam Khanna reiterated a Buy rating but cut his price target to $330 from $350, representing a potential 15.09% upside.
  • Five-star RBC Capital analyst Kenneth Herbert maintained a Buy rating and a $355 price target, suggesting a possible 23.81% upside.
  • Five-star Jefferies analyst Sheila Kahyaoglu kept a Buy rating and a $375 price target, implying a potential 30.79% upside.
  • Five-star Bank of America Securities analyst Ronald Epstein stuck with a Buy rating and a $365 price target, representing a possible 27.30% upside.
  • Four-star UBS analyst Gavin Parsons reiterated a Buy rating but lowered his price target to $350 from $357, suggesting a potential 22.07% upside.

Khanna's updated coverage of GE Aerospace stock cited the company's latest earnings report. He noted that earnings visibility and conservative planning affected his decision to stick with a Buy rating but lower his price target for GE stock.

GE Aerospace Stock Movement Today

GE Aerospace stock was down 0.36% on Wednesday, extending a 7.12% year-to-date fall. Even so, the shares have increased 48.04% over the past 12 months.

GE stock trading activity today was limited, as some 22,000 shares changed hands. This is well below the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 5.48 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Dow Jones Rebounds as Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Wednesday in the green after President Trump extended the U.S.-Iran ceasefire by an unspecified period of time to allow for further negotiations.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

In a Truth Social post, Trump said the ceasefire will last "until such time as their leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal." He added that the U.S. will hold off on direct attacks against Iran while continuing to blockade vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports for the duration of the truce.

Iran Internal Divide Spurs Ceasefire Uncertainty

A source familiar with the matter told Axios that the ceasefire will last between three and five days. It was originally scheduled to end on Tuesday evening, although Trump extended the deadline by one day earlier this week.

The White House believes there is a sharp divide between Iranian negotiators and the IRGC. That was exemplified last week when Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, only to be quickly contradicted and berated by the IRGC, which signaled the waterway would remain closed.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: This Is Why Teleflex Stock (TFX) Rallied Today
data.description:
Teleflex (TFX) stock was on the rise Wednesday after a bid to take the medical device company private was revealed. A news report claimed that private equity firms CVC Capital Partners and GTCR have submitted a joint bid that would take Teleflex private. The finer details of the deal, such as the price and timing, are still unknown.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

According to insiders, Teleflex's management team is evaluating the offer from CVC Capital Partners and GTCR. However, they have warned that there is no guarantee that anything will come from this offer. These sources claimed that Teleflex may reject the offer or use it to attract similar bids from other companies.

The potential offer for Teleflex came at a time when the company was streamlining its business, including the sale of three units in December for $2.03 billion. Teleflex has also faced scrutiny from investors, including Irenic Capital Management, after it pushed back against calls for a sale of the company.

Teleflex Stock Movement Today

Teleflex stock was up 9.37% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a 5.46% fall yesterday. The shares have rallied 2.52% year-to-date but were still down 6.22% over the past 12 months.

TFX stock trading activity was muted this morning despite the news of a potential deal to take the company private. As of this writing, roughly 67,000 shares have changed hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 745,000 shares.
data.author: William White
